ssh_vs_https.noob:
There are two ways to connect to GitHub:

  HTTPS (easier to start):
  $ git clone https://github.com/user/repo.git
  Asks for username + token when pushing

  SSH (better long-term):
  $ git clone git@github.com:user/repo.git
  Never asks for password once set up

  For beginners: use HTTPS first, switch to SSH later.
ssh_vs_https.learner:
HTTPS: use token in URL or prompt. SSH: one-time key setup then password-free forever.
  Set up SSH:
  $ ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C 'email'
  $ cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub  # add to GitHub Settings
ssh_vs_https.pro: HTTPS with PAT in remote URL or SSH key. ed25519 preferred over RSA.

git_push_rejected.noob:
GitHub is rejecting your push because the online version has changes you haven't downloaded yet.

  Fix:
  1. Pull the latest changes first:
  $ git pull

  2. If there are conflicts, resolve them, then:
  $ git add .
  $ git commit -m 'merge'
  $ git push
git_push_rejected.learner:
Non-fast-forward: remote has commits you don't have locally.
  $ git pull --rebase  # cleaner than merge
  Or: $ git pull && git push
  Force push only if you know what you're doing: git push --force-with-lease
git_push_rejected.pro: git pull --rebase then push. Or git push --force-with-lease if sure.

detached_head.noob:
You're in a 'detached HEAD' state — you're viewing an old version of your project, not the current branch.

  To get back to normal:
  $ git checkout main
  (or 'master' if that's your main branch)
detached_head.learner:
Detached HEAD means HEAD points to a commit, not a branch.
  Return to branch: git checkout main
  Save work from detached HEAD: git checkout -b new-branch-name
detached_head.pro: git checkout main. To save: git checkout -b branch-name from detached state.
